name: V6 Cylinder Block (1.6L 90° Bank)
notes: 80mm bore, 90° bank angle per FIA regs. K-Plating applied (Kumamoto method) to bore surface

name: Crankshaft
notes: Connects to MGU-K via gear. Must balance torsional resonance (key failure mode 2015–2017)

name: Connecting Rods (x6)
notes: Ultra-lightweight; 6 cylinders, each 150g. Tolerance <0.01mm

name: Pistons (x6)
notes: 3D-printed steel pistons (additive mfg) used from RA620H 2020. Complex internal ribbing

name: Piston Rings (x18)
notes: 3 rings per piston; K-Plating bore reduces ring wear at high combustion pressure

name: Cylinder Head (x2 banks)
notes: 32° valve angle, flat-top combustion chamber for rapid combustion (RA621H 2021 redesign)

name: Intake Valves (x12)
notes: 2 intake valves per cylinder. Minimum stem diameter 5mm per FIA regs

name: Exhaust Valves (x12)
notes: High-temp alloy; exhaust exits outside bank (per regs). Feeds turbine

name: Camshafts (x4)
notes: 2 DOHC per bank. Drives valve timing. Pneumatic valve springs (not mechanical)

name: Valve Actuation – Pneumatic System
notes: Replaces coil springs; enables 20,000+ rpm without valve float

name: Engine Block Lower Casing
notes: Structural load path for PU mounting to gearbox

name: Direct Injection System (x6 injectors)
notes: Max injection pressure 500 bar per FIA regs. Critical for new combustion process (2019+)

name: Ignition System (x6 coils + spark plugs)
notes: Synchronised with MGU-H energy recovery for combustion efficiency

name: Exhaust Manifold (equal-length runners)
notes: Individual equal-length runners reworked RA617H+. Feeds turbine efficiently

name: Engine Assembly & Bench Calibration
notes: Full ICE assembly + dyno calibration at Sakura HRC facility

name: Turbine Housing
notes: Additive manufactured from 2020. Rear-projecting layout (RA617H+ redesign)

name: Turbine Blades / Wheel
notes: 3D complex blade geometry for high efficiency. HondaJet gas turbine knowledge applied

name: Compressor Housing
notes: Front-projecting (RA617H redesign). Freed from V-bank; larger size for higher airflow

name: Compressor Wheel
notes: Wide pressure ratio coverage. Redesigned 2019 French GP for efficiency across full range

name: Turbo Shaft (MGU-H Drive Shaft)
notes: Connects turbine-MGU-H-compressor. Changed to longer shaft 2017 for turbine efficiency

name: Turbo Bearing Assembly
notes: Changed greased→oil lubrication RA618H. Pressurized chamber to seal oil at 100k+ rpm

name: Charge Air Cooler (Inter-cooler)
notes: Cools compressed air before intake. Compact design per McLaren era (RA615H)

name: Wastegate Actuator
notes: Bypasses excess exhaust; less critical with MGU-H managing boost

name: Turbocharger Sub-Assembly
notes: Full turbo unit assembled before mating with MGU-H

name: MGU-H Rotor & Magnet Assembly
notes: RA621H: high magnetic flux density magnets + high thermal conductivity insulators

name: MGU-H Stator Windings
notes: Converts shaft rotation→electricity (gen mode) or electricity→shaft (motor mode)

name: MGU-H Housing
notes: Pressurized chamber for oil sealing. Sandwiched between turbine & compressor

name: MGU-H Cooling Circuit
notes: Maintains thermal limits at 125,000 rpm. Critical for RA621H output targets

name: MGU-H Assembly (complete)
notes: Mated to turbo shaft. Min weight 4kg. Power ~90-100 kW at 125,000 rpm

name: MGU-K Rotor & Magnet Assembly
notes: RA621H gear ratio change allows energy recovery at even lower engine speeds

name: MGU-K Stator Windings
notes: Max output 120 kW / 200 Nm per FIA regs. Housing rigidity increased RA621H for vibration

name: MGU-K Gear Interface
notes: Connects MGU-K to crankshaft. Shaft weakness (spring design) resolved torsional resonance

name: MGU-K Housing & Bearing
notes: Bearing support changed 2018; radial load allowance increased after bench-vs-installed failure

name: MGU-K Assembly (complete)
notes: Connected to crankshaft gear. Provides up to 160 bhp ERS deployment

name: Li-Ion Battery Cells (ES)
notes: High-performance specialist battery. Max 4 MJ deploy per lap / 2 MJ recover via MGU-K

name: Battery Management System (BMS)
notes: Manages thermal limits, charge/discharge rate, and voltage balance

name: ES Thermal Management
notes: Fast charge/discharge generates extreme heat. Integrated liquid cooling

name: Power Electronics / Inverter Unit
notes: Bi-directional AC/DC converters. Enables direct MGU-H→MGU-K routing bypassing battery

name: Dry-Sump Oil Tank
notes: Low-profile for CoG; separated from engine block

name: Oil Pump & Scavenge Pumps
notes: Multiple scavenge pumps for high-G cornering oil control

name: Oil Cooler
notes: Keeps oil at optimal viscosity; integrated into cooling circuit

name: Oil Filter & Pressure Regulators
notes: Prevents particulate damage. Critical for engine life management (2–3 races per PU)

name: Water Pump (Electric)
notes: Electrically driven for independent cooling control

name: Radiators (x2 side pods)
notes: Side pod mounted. Compact design to minimise aerodynamic drag

name: Coolant Hoses & Distribution Lines
notes: Routes coolant to ICE, MGU-K, ES. High-temp rated >180°C

name: Fuel Tank (FIA Bladder, ~100kg capacity)
notes: Crash-tested safety bladder. Positioned centrally for weight distribution

name: Fuel Pump & Pressure Regulator
notes: Must supply 100 kg/hr max flow rate (FIA regulated). High-pressure to injectors

name: Fuel Filter & Lines
notes: Prevents injector damage. Zero tolerance for particulate contamination

name: Engine Control Unit (ECU) – FIA Standard
notes: FIA mandated McLaren TAG-320 ECU. Controls all PU functions + energy management

name: Wiring Loom (PU section)
notes: ~1 km total wiring in F1 car. PU section manages MGU-H, MGU-K, ES, ICE signals

name: Sensors Array (temp/pressure/speed – PU)
notes: 100s of sensors. Data telemetered live to Sakura HRC during race

name: Carbon Fibre Monocoque (Survival Cell)
notes: Autoclave-cured CFRP. Accuracy ±0.05mm. Integrates survival cell, fuel tank cavity

name: Roll Hoop (Halo + Main)
notes: Halo (titanium, 7.5mm thick) mandatory since 2018. Withstands 125 kN load

name: Ballast Weights
notes: Adjust CoG and minimum weight (798 kg with driver 2021). Critical for balance

name: Mounting Points – Engine & Gearbox
notes: Engine stressed member of chassis. PU acts as structural element

name: Front Upper Wishbone (x2)
notes: Double wishbone geometry. Push-rod actuation layout front

name: Front Lower Wishbone (x2)
notes: Aero-shaped cross-section to manage underfloor airflow

name: Front Push-Rod (x2)
notes: Transmits suspension loads to inboard rocker

name: Front Rockers & Torsion Bars (x2)
notes: Adjustable setup for different circuit characteristics (stiff vs compliant)

name: Front Upright & Hub Assembly (x2)
notes: Houses wheel bearing; interfaces with brake caliper and wheel

name: Front Anti-Roll Bar
notes: Controls body roll in cornering. Adjustable stiffness

name: Rear Upper Wishbone (x2)
notes: Pull-rod rear actuation layout (RB16B). Lower CoG benefit

name: Rear Lower Wishbone (x2)
notes: Designed around gearbox casing geometry

name: Rear Pull-Rod (x2)
notes: Loads fed down to gearbox-mounted damper unit

name: Rear Rockers & Damper Unit (x2)
notes: Gearbox integrated. Complex multi-element damper

name: Rear Upright & Hub Assembly (x2)
notes: Interfaces with brake-by-wire system and driveshaft

name: Rear Anti-Roll Bar
notes: Adjustable; key setup parameter for rear stability

name: Front Wing Mainplane
notes: Most complex aero part. 4–6 weeks design+test. Multiple elements for downforce

name: Front Wing Flap Cascade Elements (x4)
notes: Adjustable for downforce level. Replaced each race weekend after contact risk

name: Nose Cone (crash structure)
notes: FIA crash-tested. Sacrificial structure. Shaped for aerodynamic entry

name: Bargeboard / Vane Package
notes: Directs airflow to sidepods and floor. High-update rate component across season

name: Floor & Skid Block Assembly
notes: Generates significant ground effect downforce. FIA-regulated 10mm wooden plank

name: Rear Wing Mainplane
notes: Fixed structure. Angle adjusted per circuit (Monaco high vs Monza low downforce)

name: DRS Flap (Drag Reduction System)
notes: FIA-controlled actuation. Opens on designated DRS zones to reduce drag ~20%

name: Beam Wing
notes: Lower secondary element below rear wing. Seals diffuser exit flow

name: Diffuser
notes: Accelerates underfloor airflow exit. Critical ground-effect component

name: Gearbox Casing (8-speed semi-auto)
notes: 8-speed seamless shift. Acts as structural rear suspension carrier (RB16B)

name: Gear Stack & Selector Mechanism
notes: Seamless shift: next gear pre-engaged. <5ms shift time. Barrel cam selection

name: Differential
notes: Multi-plate clutch diff. Adjustable ramp angles for traction balance

name: Driveshafts (x2)
notes: Constant-velocity joints for suspension articulation + high-torque delivery

name: Clutch Assembly
notes: Paddle-operated. Must accommodate MGU-K torque delivery during race start

name: Carbon Brake Discs – Front (x2)
notes: ~1000°C operational temp. 28mm thick. Glowing red during braking visible in races

name: Carbon Brake Discs – Rear (x2)
notes: Brake-by-wire rear (coordinates with MGU-K regen braking)

name: Brake Calipers – Front (x2)
notes: 6-piston monoblock caliper. Custom made per team specification

name: Brake Calipers – Rear (x2)
notes: Integrated brake-by-wire actuator for regen coordination

name: Brake Ducts & Cooling Scoops (x4)
notes: Circuit-specific. Minimal cooling (low drag) at Monza; maximum at Monaco

name: Master Cylinders & Brake-by-Wire ECU
notes: Blends hydraulic front brakes with electric rear braking from MGU-K

name: Forged Magnesium Wheels – Front (x2)
notes: 18-inch (from 2022 regs). Centre-lock. OZ Racing supplied

name: Forged Magnesium Wheels – Rear (x2)
notes: Wider than front. Must withstand 12,000 Nm under heavy acceleration

name: Pirelli P Zero Tyres – set (4)
notes: 5 compounds: C1–C5. Teams receive 13 sets per race weekend (2021 regs)

name: Halo Titanium Structure
notes: Mandatory since 2018. 125 kN frontal load requirement. Saved Leclerc, Zhou lives

name: Driver Seat (custom moulded)
notes: Moulded to specific driver. Removes in <5s for rescue. HANS device compatible

name: Steering Wheel & Column
notes: ~25 buttons, rotary dials. Detachable; driver must exit with it per FIA regs

name: Safety Harness (6-point)
notes: 6-point HANS-compatible. Replaced each race event

name: Fire Suppression System
notes: Cockpit + engine bay nozzles. Driver-activated or auto-trigger

name: Drink System & Helmet Cooling
notes: Delivers electrolyte drink + cooling air inside helmet during race

name: PU Installation into Chassis
notes: Honda RA621H power unit (ICE+MGU-H+MGU-K+ES+inverter) installed as structural member

name: Gearbox Mating to PU
notes: Gearbox bolted to PU rear face. Rear suspension carrier now active

name: Suspension Assembly – Full Car
notes: Front and rear suspension installed. Geometry set to base setup

name: Aero & Bodywork Installation
notes: All carbon bodywork, wings, floor fitted. Circuit-specific aero configuration

name: Brakes, Wheels & Tyre Fitting
notes: Braking system installed. Brake-by-wire calibrated. Tyres fitted and pressured

name: Cockpit, Safety & Electronics Commissioning
notes: Driver seat, halo, steering, safety systems installed. ECU flashed with race software

name: Full Car Systems Check & Sign-Off
notes: Full car fired up, all PU systems verified, weight/CoG measured. FIA scrutineering ready
